DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Ficenec, Esq. 925-930-6600 of Archer Norris for Plaintiff Kinson Wong.
Sanjeet Ganjam, Esq. 415-421-6500 of Shartsis Friese LLP for Defendant JOE LING.
Jenny Jung, Esq. 650-422-2130 of LTL Attorneys for Defendants THOMAS WONG, C.K. KWAN, EDMOND HON, 633 PARTNERSHIP, AND 633 HOLDINGS, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. Both sides Argue. Defendant argues standing, statute of limitations.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT THOMAS WONG, C.K. KWAN, EDMOND HON, 633 PARTNERSHIP, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL PARTNERSHIP, 633 HOLDINGS, INC. DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

efendants Wai Kun Wong, 633 Partnership, 633 Holdings, Inc., C.K. Kwan and Edmond Hon's demurrer to the first cause of action for fraud in the inducement and rescission in the third amended complaint (TAC) filed by plaintiff Kinson Wong individually and derivatively on behalf of R & G Executive Lounge, Inc. is OVERRULED in its entirety.

As Judge Kahn has already ruled in a related case, Kinton Wong has standing to bring both a derivative and direct action against defendants. Fraudulent inducement is pled with the required specificity and is not time barred. Kinson Wong sufficiently pleads facts to show the time and manner of discovery and the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence.

The TAC also adequately alleges that (1) Ling's misrepresentations bind 633 Partnership, since Ling was general partner and acting with apparent authority when making them; (2) 633 Holdings, Inc. has successor liability because 633 Holdings, Inc. is purportedly a continuation of 633 Partnership and was formed to avoid 633 Partnership's liability and (3) Wai Kun Wong, Kwan, and Hon are properly named defendants because they benefited from Ling's misrepresentations through the receipt of excessive rents and property improvements paid by R&G.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR#7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Ficenec, Esq. 925-930-6600 of Archer Norris for Plaintiff Kinson Wong.
Sanjeet Ganjam, Esq. 415-421-6500 of Shartsis Friese LLP for Defendant JOE LING.
Jenny Jung, Esq. 650-422-2130 of LTL Attorneys for Defendants THOMAS WONG, C.K. KWAN, EDMOND HON, 633 PARTNERSHIP, AND 633 HOLDINGS, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. Both sides Argue.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOE LING's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Joe Ling's demurrer to the first cause of action for fraud in the inducement and rescission in the third amended complaint (TAC) filed by Kinson Wong individually and derivatively on behalf of R&G Executive Lounge, Inc. is OVERRULED. Ling's demurrers to the second and third causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty and accounting are SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND.

The fraudulent inducement claim is not time barred. The TAC sufficiently pleads facts to show the time and manner of discovery and the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence.

Kinson Wong has standing to bring a breach of fiduciary duty claim as a minority shareholder of Gold Mountain Restaurant against the majority shareholder, Ling. The statute of repose under Corporations Code 2011(a)(2) does not apply. (See Penasquitos, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1991) 53 Cal.3d 1180, 1191-92.) The TAC sufficiently alleges that the restaurant corporation was not properly dissolved because the liquor license was not sold. However, the demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to allow Kinson Wong to allege facts showing that, at the time he signed the certificate of dissolution, he did not know Gold Mountain continued to possess the liquor license. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR#7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)
